message:
fix(tests,bd-cxhqb): replace fragile mtime-based rebuild assertions with content fingerprints

Bead coding_agent_session_search-cxhqb (adversarial-tester finding).

Two tests compared filesystem directory/file mtimes across a cass
rebuild boundary, paired with wall-clock sleeps, to decide whether
cass did a real rebuild (Site 1) or preserved a no-op checkpoint
(Site 2). Both are fragile on filesystems with coarse mtime
granularity (FAT32 has 2-second, some NFS setups have 1-second),
and the sleeps waste wall-clock time on every test run.

Site 1 — tests/e2e_search_index.rs::force_rebuild_recreates_index

Replaced `fs::metadata(index_dir).modified()` before/after with a
content fingerprint: walkdir-sorted list of (relative path, file
size) tuples under the index tree. Tantivy writes new segment files
(new UUIDs, different sizes) on every real rebuild, so the set is
guaranteed to differ when a rebuild actually happened — regardless
of filesystem mtime resolution. Dropped the 1-second `thread::sleep`
that was only there to defeat coarse-mtime collisions.

Site 2 — tests/cli_index.rs::repeat_full_json_preserves_exact_totals_when_noop_scan_underreports

Replaced `fs::metadata(checkpoint_path).modified()` before/after
with a byte-for-byte comparison of the checkpoint JSON content
(`fs::read`). This is both stricter (catches content rewrites that
yield identical post-write mtimes but differ internally — e.g. a
regression that rewrites the same fields with new `updated_at_ms`)
AND filesystem-granularity-independent. Dropped the 5ms
`thread::sleep` that was a fragile defense against same-resolution
mtime collisions.

Verified locally:
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p3 \
  rch exec -- cargo test --test e2e_search_index force_rebuild_recreates_index
  -> 1 passed in 3.00s

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p3 \
  rch exec -- cargo test --test cli_index repeat_full_json_preserves_exact_totals_when_noop_scan_underreports
  -> 1 passed in 3.05s

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
